tagline: A temporal loyalty cascade where one man rewrites gang economics — and every rewrite costs someone their future.
visualizationReason: Tokyo Revengers is fundamentally a temporal feedback loop: each time leap changes the causal graph, creating new winners and losers. Only a timeline renderer captures how intervention points cascade.

headerFlavor.loreQuote: I'm gonna save you, Mikey. No matter how many times I have to jump.
headerFlavor.sysWarning: // [SYS_OP]: TIME LEAP PROTOCOL ACTIVE — PARADOXICAL INTERVENTION IN PROGRESS

hero.microHook: I can travel through time — but only by watching people die.
hero.thesis: Takemichi's time leaps are a loyalty cascade: each intervention redistributes gang power, but the cost is always someone else's future.

aiInsights.casual: Tokyo Revengers is a time-travel gang management sim — Takemichi jumps back to save Mikey, but each intervention reshuffles who wins.
aiInsights.deep: Takemichi is a loyalty arbitrage mechanism: each time leap redistributes gang power toward Mikey, but the cost is always someone elses future.

systemQuestions.question: How does the power system in Tokyo Revengers work?
systemQuestions.answer: A temporal loyalty cascade where one man rewrites gang economics — and every rewrite costs someone their future.

systemQuestions.question: What makes Tokyo Revengers's world unique?
systemQuestions.answer: Tokyo Revengers is fundamentally a temporal feedback loop: each time leap changes the causal graph, creating new winners and losers. Only a timeline renderer captures how intervention points cascade.
